steps.title: Map the passage’s two-part structure
steps.content: The first sentence names two specific buildings (ARK and House in Goido) as examples of a trend — juxtaposing metal with traditional organic materials. The second sentence explains where that trend came from: post-WWII emphasis on technological progress and longstanding aesthetic traditions. So the structure is: examples first, underlying principles second.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice against that structure
steps.content:
• Choice A — The text distinguishes between two aesthetic approaches to architecture and then submits that one approach has had more of a long-term impact than the other has had.: The text never ranks or compares the two approaches by impact; it presents them as coexisting. ✗
  • Choice B — The text names projects that are noteworthy for their inclusion of certain materials and then explains past important uses of the materials.: The second sentence explains historical principles driving the trend, not “past uses” of metal or wood. ✗
  • Choice C — The text cites examples of a design trend and then briefly establishes the principles underlying the trend.: Matches exactly — two buildings as examples, then the post-WWII and traditional-preservation principles behind them. ✓
  • Choice D — The text introduces the salient characteristics of two buildings and then details the historical events that occasioned the buildings' designs.: Overstates the second sentence; the text gives broad historical context for a trend, not events that specifically caused these two buildings. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
